cb_layout: c0=cb_in c1=cb_scaler c2=cb_eps c3=cb_gamma_rm c4=cb_gamma c16=cb_out c24=cb_x c25=cb_xsq c26=cb_rms c27=cb_rsqrt c28=cb_normed

description: Identity passthrough: reader + tilize(RM)/copy + untilize(RM) + writer for both layouts

approach: RM path: TensorAccessor stick reads, 32 sticks per tile-row, push Wt pages. TILE path: read Wt tiles one-at-a-time. No scaler/eps/gamma for stage 1.
RM path: compute_kernel_hw_startup + tilize(cb_in,cb_x) + untilize<RMS_Wt>(cb_x,cb_out). TILE path: copy_tile loop cb_in->cb_out one tile at a time.
RM path: wait Wt pages, extract 32 sticks via TensorAccessor noc_async_write. TILE path: write Wt tiles one-at-a-time via TensorAccessor.

change: Fixed tile_size to always use ttnn.tile_size(dtype) instead of buffer_page_size for non-RM
reason: tile_size must be consistent tile size for all CB page sizes, not RM stick size

change: Fixed cb_in page_size from input_page_size to tile_size
reason: RM path pushes Wt tile-sized pages, not stick-sized pages. CB page_size must match kernel push/pop semantics.

change: Fixed cb_out page_size from output_page_size to tile_size
reason: RM path untilize produces Wt tile-sized pages. CB page_size must match kernel push/pop semantics.

change: Made stick_size layout-aware: RM uses W*elem_size, TILE uses tile_size
reason: TensorAccessor page_size and noc_async_read byte count must match actual page size for each layout

cb_summary: RM: c0:Wt/Wt c24:Wt/Wt c16:Wt/Wt. TILE: c0:1/1 c16:1/1. All balanced.

description: Square input + reduce SUM with 1/W scaler = mean(x^2). Output is reduced shape [..,H,32] with valid col 0.

approach: Added prepare_reduce_scaler<cb_scaler>(1.0f/(Wt*32)) for mean computation. Single push per row (no double-push yet).
Added square(compute_input_cb, cb_xsq) + reduce<SUM,REDUCE_ROW>(cb_xsq, cb_scaler, cb_rms). RM: untilize<1,cb_rms,cb_out>. TILE: copy_tile cb_rms->cb_out. Persistent scaler wait/pop.

change: Added output_Wt = output_W // 32 for reduced-shape stages
reason: Writer Wt must match output tensor width, not input width, for reduced-shape intermediate stages

change: cb_out total_size uses output_Wt instead of Wt
reason: Reduced-shape output has fewer tiles per row than input

change: Writer uses output_stick_size and output_Wt instead of input stick_size and Wt
reason: Reduced-shape output has different W than input, writer must match output dimensions

change: Output shape changed to list(shape[:-1]) + [32] for reduced-shape stage 2
reason: Stage 2 produces reduced output with W=32 (1 tile width from REDUCE_ROW)

cb_summary: RM: c0:Wt/Wt c24:Wt/Wt(sq) c25:1/1(streaming) c26:1/1 c16:1/1. TILE: c0:1/1(sq) c25:1/1 c26:1/1 c16:1/1. c1:persist(wait+pop). All balanced.

details: TypeError: ttnn.Shape does not support slice indexing. input_tensor.shape[:-1] fails.

description: ttnn.Shape does not support Python slice syntax [:-1]. Need to convert to list first before slicing.
evidence: TypeError: __getitem__(): incompatible function arguments - slice not supported

change: Use list comprehension [shape[i] for i in range(len-1)] instead of shape[:-1] slice to avoid ttnn.Shape slice error

triage: TRISC0=Done TRISC1=W TRISC2=W NCRISC=W BRISC=NTW on core(0,0)
suspected_cause: compute_kernel_hw_startup args wrong or missing proper init for square/reduce helpers

description: compute_kernel_hw_startup needs correct CB args. For TILE path compute_input_cb=cb_in=0, but square helper reads cb_in. Also the copy_tile at end of TILE path lacks copy_tile_to_dst_init_short. The real fix: use binary_op_init_common as design says, and add proper init for copy_tile.
evidence: TRISC0=Done TRISC1/2=Wait suggests bad math/pack init state

change: TILE path: reduce outputs directly to cb_out instead of cb_rms+copy_tile. Removed manual copy_tile that lacked proper init. RM path: reduce to cb_rms then untilize to cb_out.

triage: 1x1x32x32 TILE PASSED, 1x1x64x128 TILE HUNG on 2nd row
suspected_cause: reduce helper pops scaler after each row, but scaler pushed only once by reader

description: Reduce helper with default WaitAndPopPerTile policy pops scaler CB after each call. Scaler pushed once by reader, so 2nd row hangs waiting for scaler. Fix: remove manual cb_wait/pop for scaler and let reduce manage it, or use a persistent policy. The reduce API default is WaitAndPopPerTile for input, but scaler may have separate handling. Need to check reduce_helpers_compute.inl to understand scaler lifetime.

change: Changed compute_kernel_hw_startup to match first operation CBs: (compute_input_cb, compute_input_cb, cb_xsq). Removed manual cb_wait_front/cb_pop_front for scaler - reduce helper handles it.

description: Full RMS norm without gamma: add_eps + rsqrt + 2nd tilize + mul_col + untilize. Output matches input shape.

approach: Added cb_normed=28 declaration. Fixed RM path: final_tile_cb changed from cb_x to cb_normed to avoid in-place deadlock. Untilize changed from cb_x to cb_normed. Added phases 4 (add+rsqrt), 5 (2nd tilize), 6 (mul_col), 8 (untilize). TILE path writes directly to cb_out.

fix: Added epsilon bit-cast (struct.pack/unpack) as RT arg index 5. Allocated cb_normed always (not just for gamma). normed_pages=Wt for no-gamma RM path.
Changed output shape from reduced [..,32] back to input_tensor.shape for full normalization output.

approach: Added epsilon generation via prepare_reduce_scaler<cb_eps>. Added double-push loop (pass=0..1) reading same row twice. Added eps_u32 RT arg at index 5.

summary: All CBs balanced for both RM and TILE paths. RM: cb_in(Wt) 2x push/pop per row, cb_x(Wt) 2x push/pop, cb_xsq(Wt) 1x, cb_rms(1) 1x, cb_eps(1) persistent, cb_scaler(1) persistent, cb_rsqrt(1) 1x, cb_normed(Wt) 1x push/pop, cb_out(Wt) 1x. TILE: cb_in(Wt) streaming 1-at-a-time 2x passes, cb_xsq(Wt) 1x, cb_rms(1) 1x, cb_rsqrt(1) 1x, cb_out(Wt) streaming.
sfpu_init.h does not exist. rsqrt.h had wrong include path (compute_kernel_api/ vs api/compute/).

hypothesis: Two bad includes from upstream: sfpu_init.h does not exist (remove it), and rsqrt.h should be api/compute/eltwise_unary/rsqrt.h not compute_kernel_api/eltwise_unary/rsqrt.h.

fix: Removed non-existent sfpu_init.h include. Changed rsqrt.h include from compute_kernel_api/eltwise_unary/rsqrt.h to api/compute/eltwise_unary/rsqrt.h.

description: Full RMS norm with gamma: adds mul_row(gamma) after normalize. Reader tilizes gamma RM data. Output matches input shape.

approach: Added cb_gamma_rm/cb_gamma declarations. Added gamma tilize at program start (tilize<cb_gamma_rm,cb_gamma>). Added mul_col_out_cb routing: HAS_GAMMA->cb_normed, else->final_tile_cb. Added Phase 7 mul<ROW>(cb_normed,cb_gamma,final_tile_cb). Updated final_tile_cb: RM+gamma=cb_x, RM+no_gamma=cb_normed, TILE=cb_out. Updated untilize source: RM+gamma=cb_x, RM+no_gamma=cb_normed. Added cb_gamma pop at cleanup.

Added gamma staging under HAS_GAMMA: reserve Wt pages in cb_gamma_rm, zero-fill with MEM_ZEROS_BASE, read gamma stick at row 0 using get_noc_addr(gamma_addr), push Wt pages. Gamma is tilized by compute.

Added gamma TensorAccessor with chained CT arg index (next_compile_time_args_offset). Added gamma_page_size CT arg. Replaced raw get_noc_addr(gamma_addr) with gamma_accessor.get_noc_addr(0). Zero-fill with MEM_ZEROS_BASE, then read gamma stick at row 0.

change: Added gamma_page_size CT arg and gamma TensorAccessor CT args to reader_ct_args when has_gamma.
reason: Reader kernel needs TensorAccessor for gamma DRAM reads and gamma page size for zero-fill sizing.

hypothesis: cb_normed with 1 page for gamma path will deadlock. mul_col pushes 1 tile at a time (OutputPerTile), but mul_row has not started yet (same compute thread, sequential). After push 1, reserve_back for tile 2 blocks because cb_normed is full. Need Wt pages for cb_normed in gamma path too.

fix: Changed cb_normed pages from Wt only for no-gamma RM to Wt for any path using cb_normed as intermediate (is_input_rm or has_gamma). Prevents sequential compute deadlock.
reason: mul_col and mul_row both run on same compute thread, so cb_normed must hold all Wt tiles before mul_row can start consuming.

summary: All CBs balanced for both RM+gamma and TILE+gamma paths. cb_normed now has Wt pages for gamma path (sequential compute phases). cb_gamma_rm(Wt) one-shot push/consume by tilize. cb_gamma(Wt) persistent. cb_x reused 3 times per row in RM+gamma (tilize-square, tilize-mul_col, mul_row-untilize). All push/pop counts match.
